briefs.title: Liquor Licence Renewal Fees Rising for First Time Since 2010 — Effective May 1, 2026
briefs.summary: BC's Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) is raising annual liquor licence renewal fees starting May 1, 2026 — the first increase in over 15 years. For most hospitality businesses in the lowest fee tier, the change means roughly $75 more per year. Manufacturer fees vary by production size. The Serving It Right and Special Event Server training programs will also cost $5 more.
briefs.action_required: Check your fee tier on the LCRB fee table and budget for higher renewal costs by May 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC LCRB liquor licence renewal fee increase takes effect
BC Serving It Right and Special Event Server training fee increase takes effect
